data.title: After the Bybit attack, the total liquidation amount of the entire network reached 325 million US dollars, and the liquidation amount of ETH was about 87.9 million US dollars
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Bybit was disclosed to have been hacked at around 23:30 last night, and nearly $1.5 billion in funds were stolen. It has been about 12 hours so far. According to Coinglass data, the total amount of liquidation in the past 12 hours reached $325 million, of which long orders were liquidated for $265 million, short orders were liquidated for $59.4751 million, and ETH liquidation amounted to about $87.9 million.

data.title: Bybit CEO: I didn’t sleep all night and focused on the meeting. I learned about the hacker attack at around 10 pm on the 21st.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Bybit co-founder and CEO BenZhou posted on the X platform: "Some people saw me wearing a WHOOP watch (in the online live broadcast in the early morning) and asked me what the stress monitor looked like last night. This is the monitoring result. I hardly slept, but it actually looks pretty good. I think I may be too focused on handling all the meetings and forget to decompress. I feel that when I really understand the concept of losing $1.5 billion, it will come soon. Please note that I learned about the hacker attack around 10 o'clock in the evening."

data.title: ‘Demolish the Ministry of Finance!’ protest erupts in Tokyo, angry attendees say ‘we are not your ATM’
data.description:
Angry residents of Japan fed up with the country’s monetary recklessness gathered outside the Ministry of Finance in Tokyo on Friday evening. Shouting “dismantle the finance ministry!” attendees brandished signs saying things like “we are not your ATM” and mocking prime minister Shigeru Ishiba.  Compared to other demonstrations and protests around the world, Friday evening’s […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/ministry-of-finance-protest-tokyo/

data.title: Bybit gets 350k withdrawal requests following the hack, receives 40,000 ETH loan from Bitget
data.description:
Earlier today, Bybit lost $1.5 billion in digital assets after one of its cold wallets got compromised. Following the incident, Bybit received over 350K customer withdrawal requests. In an X post, CEO Ben Zhou mentioned that 99.99 percent of the withdrawal requests have been processed with only 2100 remaining.  He asked the customers with completed […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/bybit-gets-350k-withdrawal-requests/

data.title: SEC Lawsuit Against OpenSea Ends Amid Regulatory Shift
data.description:
SEC Lawsuit Against OpenSea has been dropped, marking a key moment for NFTs. The decision comes as OpenSea moves forward with the launch of its SEA token.
source: https://coincu.com/323587-sec-lawsuit-against-opensea-ends

data.title: Security Breach Suspected In Safe Owner Signature Attack
data.description: According to Odaily, SlowMist CISO 23pds reported on the X platform that attackers managed to seize safe owner permissions through a single forged signature attack. It is suspected that multiple macOS or Windows computers were compromised. The attackers might have been present in the internal network for some time, potentially monitoring internal communications and transaction timings.

data.title: Bybit attackers may have hacked into the official staff's computer and conducted internal network monitoring to carry out the attack
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: SlowMist CISO 23pds posted on X platform: "The attacker took away the safe owner privilege with a forged signature attack. It is speculated that more than one macOS or Windows computer must have been controlled, and the attacker may have stayed in the intranet for a while and was able to monitor internal chats, transfer times and other information."

data.title: Bitget CEO: The 40,000 ETH previously transferred to Bybit are all the platform’s own funds and have nothing to do with user assets
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Bitget CEO Gracy Chen posted on the X platform: "All ETH previously transferred from Bitget to Bybit were its own funds. In the current difficult period, we are also assisting in tracking and investigating stolen funds. All Bitget users' assets are safely stored on our platform, and we issue proof of reserve (PoR) every month to ensure that the reserve ratio is greater than 1:1."

data.title: DWF Labs Partner Calls For Thorough Investigation Into Bybit Hack
data.description: According to Odaily, Andrei Grachev, a partner at DWF Labs, expressed concerns on the X platform regarding the severity of the recent security breach at Bybit. Grachev emphasized the need for a comprehensive investigation into the incident. He noted that DWF Labs has not withdrawn any funds from Bybit_Official and is prepared to offer ETH support. Additionally, Grachev expressed curiosity about the actions of Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin, recalling his role in advocating for an ETH rollback following The DAO fork incident a decade ago.

data.title: Bybit CEO: All withdrawals have been processed, the withdrawal system has returned to normal, and a full report will be released later
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Bybit co-founder and CEO Ben Zhou posted on X platform, "12 hours after the worst hacker attack in history. All withdrawals have been processed. Bybit withdrawal system is now fully back to normal, users can withdraw any amount without any delay. Thank you for your patience, Bybit is sorry for this situation. Bybit will release a full incident report and security measures in the next few days. We will continue to inform you of updates. Thank you to all the customers, friends and partners who helped us and supported us during this torturous 12 hours.
The real work has just begun."
